item.title: REXONA SUVA MARATHON 2026 | Marathon supports cancer warriors
item.description: CHILDREN battling cancer in Fiji become young champions of advocating the search for joy and the essence of love and life, and for WOWS Kids Fiji, their “little champs” smiles propelled the team towards becoming a part of the Rexona Suva Marathon for this year. WOWS Kids Fiji is the charity partner of the event this year and its board member Kimberlie Smart has welcomed the news of their nominatio
item.content:encoded:
CHILDREN battling cancer in Fiji become young champions of advocating the search for joy and the essence of love and life, and for WOWS Kids Fiji, their “little champs” smiles propelled the team towards becoming a part of the Rexona Suva Marathon for this year.

WOWS Kids Fiji is the charity partner of the event this year and its board member Kimberlie Smart has welcomed the news of their nomination to be the beneficiary of the marathon.

Smart, who has been part of the charity organisation since 2012, says the news of their selection is an exciting one.

In relaying the charity’s appreciation of its nomination, she said the partnership was an incredible one.

“I suppose our champs are often going through a marathon themselves in their battle with cancer, so I think it’s an incredible partnership that we have here with the Suva Marathon,” Smart told guests at the marathon’s launch yesterday.

“Our children are always so excited for anything that is community based and I know they will be very happy to be part of this new venture.”

The marathon was launched at the office of the Motibhai Group of Companies in Walu Bay, Suva and attended by representatives of the Motibhai Group, WOWS Kids Fiji and Suva Marathon Club.

Smart added the event would be a vital platform to highlight the plight of children afflicted with cancer.

“This will not only be about the dedications that may come to WOWS from this, but just getting together and a community opportunity to be able to create awareness for childhood cancer in Fiji,” she said.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: From soccer dreamer to rising rugby player
item.description: WHAT began as a dream that seemed out of reach has quickly turned into an inspiring reality. For 19-year-old rugby player Misi William Ottarberg, had never seriously played rugby but today, the former soccer player is making his mark with Lomaiviti Black Rugby Club and the Suva Under-20 side, proving that faith, determination and hard work can open unexpected doors.
item.content:encoded:
WHAT began as a dream that seemed out of reach has quickly turned into an inspiring reality.

For 19-year-old rugby player Misi William Ottarberg, had never seriously played rugby but today, the former soccer player is making his mark with Lomaiviti Black Rugby Club and the Suva Under-20 side, proving that faith, determination and hard work can open unexpected doors.

Hailing from Nacokaika in Naitasiri with maternal links to Navunimono in Tailevu, Ottarberg’s rugby journey has been anything but conventional.

Growing up, his dream was to attend Ratu Kadavulevu School, where he hoped to develop into a rugby player. However, financial hardship meant that dream never materialised.

Instead, he attended Muaniweni College, where soccer became his sport.

Despite missing the traditional pathway that has produced many of Fiji’s rugby stars, Ottarberg never gave up on his ambition.

“I kept praying to God, asking Him that one day I would play for one of the rugby clubs in Fiji and develop my rugby career,” he said.

After completing high school, Ottarberg enrolled at Fiji National University’s Samabula campus to study Automotive Engineering.

It was there that his rugby story truly began.

Representing FNU at the Inter-Tertiary Games last year, Ottarberg discovered a natural talent for the game. Encouraged by his performances, he quietly attended the Suva Under-20 trials without telling his family.

He expected little from the opportunity.

Instead, he received a selection that would change his life.

“I was surprised when the coaches selected me that I had made it to the Suva Under-20 team. I believe this is God’s blessing and the first step towards achieving my dream of becoming a professional rugby player.”

Now playing for Lomaiviti Black Rugby Club, Ottarberg continues to adapt to the demands of rugby after years on the soccer field.

Playing at lock, he believes his football background has become one of his greatest strengths.

“The attacking skills and awareness I learned from soccer help me in rugby, especially when contesting for the ball and reading play.”

While still in the early stages of his rugby career, Ottarberg has already set his sights on an even bigger goal—to one day wear the white jersey and represent Fiji on the international stage.

He credits his teammates at Lomaiviti Black for helping him grow in confidence and develop his game, while also acknowledging the unwavering support of his family.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: $2m budget | Rugby League targets World Cup success
item.description: THE Fiji National Rugby League (FNRL) will need more than $2 million to fund the Vodafone Fiji Bati and Vodafone Fiji Bulikula campaign at this year’s Rugby League World Cup in Australia, New Zealand and Papua New Guinea, with officials stepping up efforts to secure additional financial backing. FNRL executive chairman Rajesh Singh said the governing body had already received valuable support from
item.content:encoded:
THE Fiji National Rugby League (FNRL) will need more than $2 million to fund the Vodafone Fiji Bati and Vodafone Fiji Bulikula campaign at this year’s Rugby League World Cup in Australia, New Zealand and Papua New Guinea, with officials stepping up efforts to secure additional financial backing.

FNRL executive chairman Rajesh Singh said the governing body had already received valuable support from major sponsor Vodafone but acknowledged further assistance would be needed to ensure Fiji could compete strongly at the global showpiece.

“We are looking at a budget of a bit over $2m. We have Vodafone as our sponsor but we will try to look for more sponsors,” Singh said.

“We will also write to the Fiji National Sports Commission.”

Singh also revealed that former Fiji coach Mick Potter was close to rejoining the national set-up, a move expected to strengthen the Bati’s preparations.

“Mick (Potter) is on the verge of joining us. He is no one new to Rugby League in Fiji. He was the coach when Fiji beat New Zealand at the World Cup and as we know New Zealand is in our pool this time around.”

Potter guided Fiji during one of the country’s most memorable Rugby League World Cup campaigns, and his return would provide valuable experience as the Bati seek another deep run in the tournament.

The Fiji Bati have built an impressive reputation on the world stage, reaching the Rugby League World Cup semi-finals on three occasions in 2008, 2013 and 2017.

Their greatest achievement came during the 2017 tournament when they stunned New Zealand 4-2 in the quarter-finals to book a historic semi-final berth.

This year, Fiji faces another daunting challenge after being drawn in a tough pool alongside Australia, New Zealand and the Cook Islands.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: McCaig targets history for swimming
item.description: HANSEL McCaig will carry Fiji’s hopes in the pool at the 2026 Commonwealth Games as part of a seven-member swimming squad aiming to create history in Glasgow, Scotland. The 22-year-old swimmer, who will compete alongside Reuben Taylor, Tolu Young, Samuel Yalimaiwai, Don Younger, Anahira McCutcheon and Kelera Mudunasoko, enters the games with confidence after a strong build-up leading into the comp
item.content:encoded:
HANSEL McCaig will carry Fiji’s hopes in the pool at the 2026 Commonwealth Games as part of a seven-member swimming squad aiming to create history in Glasgow, Scotland.

The 22-year-old swimmer, who will compete alongside Reuben Taylor, Tolu Young, Samuel Yalimaiwai, Don Younger, Anahira McCutcheon and Kelera Mudunasoko, enters the games with confidence after a strong build-up leading into the competition.

McCaig said preparations have progressed well, with the team now entering the final stages of their training programme before heading to Glasgow.

“We’re in the final week of heavy training, and then we deload and taper next week. So, we should be up in our peak by Commonwealth.”

The Oceania Swimming Championships played a crucial role in McCaig’s qualification campaign, while the National Age Group Championships provided another opportunity to fine-tune his preparation.

McCaig said the Oceania Championships served as the qualifying event for the Commonwealth Games, while the national competition was used as a final preparation meet ahead of the international challenge.

With Fiji yet to secure a Commonwealth Games swimming medal, McCaig is determined to help break new ground for the sport while setting realistic targets for his campaign.

“That’s always a goal. The first step is to make the semifinals and the finals. Swimming has never won a medal there before, so it would be an honour to achieve that.”

The young swimmer believes any progress made in Glasgow will help inspire the next generation of Fijian swimmers to pursue success on the international stage.

McCaig credits his preparation to the support of his coach, who has been involved throughout his journey and helped develop his race strategy.

“It’s just trusting you’re training and your race plan and sticking to your race, not looking at what other people next to you are doing.”

His pathway into swimming began as a life-saving skill before developing into a passion that has taken him to the highest level of competition.

Beyond Glasgow, McCaig has already set his sights on the Short Course World Championships in Beijing later this year while continuing his studies.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Western Schools set for semi-final
item.description: THE race to the Western Secondary Schools Rugby Union finals heats up this Saturday as the best school teams from across the western division battle for places in their respective grade deciders at 4R Electrical Stadium in Ba. With semifinal spots on the line in the U14, U15, U16, U17 and U19 grades, fans can expect an action-packed day featuring traditional rugby powerhouses and emerging schools
item.content:encoded:
THE race to the Western Secondary Schools Rugby Union finals heats up this Saturday as the best school teams from across the western division battle for places in their respective grade deciders at 4R Electrical Stadium in Ba.

With semifinal spots on the line in the U14, U15, U16, U17 and U19 grades, fans can expect an action-packed day featuring traditional rugby powerhouses and emerging schools eager to make their mark.

One of the biggest highlights will be the U18 competition, which will be played in a round-robin format instead of straight semifinals. Defending and top contenders Natabua High School, Tilak High School, Ratu Navula College, Ba Methodist High School, Cuvu College and Ba Provincial Freebird Institute will all have the opportunity to fight for valuable points in a bid to reach the championship.

The day kicks off at 8am with the U16 clash between Cuvu College and Natabua High School on Ground One, while Nadi Muslim College takes on Ba Provincial Freebird Institute in the U14 grade on Ground Two.

Other key matches include Sigatoka Methodist College against Natabua High School in the U17 division, Ba Methodist High School facing Ba Provincial Freebird Institute, and an exciting U19 semifinal between Ratu Navula College and Nadroga Navosa Provincial College.

With school pride, provincial bragging rights and places in the finals at stake, supporters can expect fierce contests and high-quality rugby throughout the day at 4R Electrical Stadium.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Vanua, Ranadi events begin
item.description: THE Vodafone Vanua Championship and Royal Tea Ranadi Cup will kick off tomorrow, marking the beginning of another important season of Fiji Rugby’s domestic competitions. Round one will see teams from across the country return to the field as they look to make a strong start to their campaigns and build early momentum in the competition.
item.content:encoded:
THE Vodafone Vanua Championship and Royal Tea Ranadi Cup will kick off tomorrow, marking the beginning of another important season of Fiji Rugby’s domestic competitions.

Round one will see teams from across the country return to the field as they look to make a strong start to their campaigns and build early momentum in the competition.

In the Vodafone Vanua Championship, Bua will host Macuata at Bua College, while Yasawa welcomes Vatukoula at Churchill Park. Northland will face Ra in another opening round encounter.

Other fixtures will see Coastland take on Northern Bulls at Narauyaba, Nasinu meet Ovalau at Buckhurst Park, and Namosi battle Serua at Thompson.

The Royal Tea Ranadi Cup will also feature six opening round matches as women’s teams begin their push for success.

Bua will face Taveuni, Yasawa will host Vatukoula, Ra will travel to meet Northland, Coastland will battle Northern Bulls, Navosa will take on Ovalau, while Malolo will face Serua.

The second round of both competitions will be held next Tuesday, with the third round scheduled four days later as teams continue their pursuit of strong positions on the competition ladder.

Both competitions remain a key part of Fiji Rugby’s development pathway, providing opportunities for emerging players to gain valuable experience while giving provincial unions a platform to showcase their talent.

As the new season begins, teams will be aiming to lay down an early marker and build momentum towards the later stages of the competition.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: New sponsorship strengthens grassroots women’s netball across Fiji
item.description: M-PAISA has announced its first-ever sports sponsorship, backing the Netball Fiji Rally in a move aimed at strengthening grassroots women’s netball across the country. The nationwide rally will involve districts from the Central, Eastern, Western and Northern divisions, with competitions stretching from Suva and Nasinu to Sigatoka, Rakiraki, Labasa, Savusavu, Bua and Taveuni.
item.content:encoded:
M-PAISA has announced its first-ever sports sponsorship, backing the Netball Fiji Rally in a move aimed at strengthening grassroots women’s netball across the country.

The nationwide rally will involve districts from the Central, Eastern, Western and Northern divisions, with competitions stretching from Suva and Nasinu to Sigatoka, Rakiraki, Labasa, Savusavu, Bua and Taveuni.

Speaking at the launch, Digital Financial Services Pte Limited general manager said the sponsorship reflected M-PAISA’s commitment to supporting grassroots women’s sports and creating opportunities for young players.

“The rally would provide a pathway for talented players, particularly those from rural communities to progress to the national level and eventually pursue professional opportunities overseas,” he said.

“The sponsorship also aims to encourage healthy lifestyles, teamwork and mentorship while helping young girls stay engaged in sport.”

M-PAISA said it was proud to partner with Netball Fiji and its parent company, Vodafone Fiji, to help develop the sport and inspire the next generation of netballers.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: Labasa builds momentum in EPL
item.description: LABASA head coach Alvin Chand says his side must remain focused and continue improving despite making a positive start to its Extra Premier Leagus campaign. Speaking after his team’s latest victory, Chand said the result was a timely confidence booster following the disappointment of missing out on the Fiji FACT title, adding that the players are determined to build momentum, in the league.
item.content:encoded:
LABASA head coach Alvin Chand says his side must remain focused and continue improving despite making a positive start to its Extra Premier Leagus campaign.

Speaking after his team’s latest victory, Chand said the result was a timely confidence booster following the disappointment of missing out on the Fiji FACT title, adding that the players are determined to build momentum, in the league.

“The performance so far in the EPL is good,” Chand said.

“Getting a win against Nadroga is a good start after the Fiji FACT campaign, and we are on the right track as far as the league is concerned.”

Chand admitted the Fiji FACT semi-final defeat had affected the squad, with several players struggling to come to terms with missing out on the title on home soil. He believes the latest victory has helped restore confidence within the team.

“This win was very important for us. We could not win the tournament and bowed out in the semi-finals.”

“So, this victory was important to uplift the morale of our boys. Hopefully, it will improve now after we got the win.”

While pleased his team’s progress, Chand said there was no room for complacency, insisting there are still aspects of the game that need attention before the next round of fixtures.

“There are very minor things that we will work on. Our communication and coordination can improve, and that will come through a lot of combination play during training. Apart from that, I think the boys have done very well in a lot of areas.”

Chand also praised the players for responding well to tactical adjustments and maintaining their composure under pressure.

“Our message was not to panic. We were creating a lot of scoring opportunities, but the goals were not coming. We told the players to stay patient, make good decisions of goal and trust the system. The changes in our style of play gave us rewards.”

As Labasa prepares for the next phase of the competition, Chand is confident his players can continue building on their performances, provided they maintain the same work ethic and commitment shown in recent weeks.

He also thanked the club’s management, coaching staff, fitness trainer, players and their families for their continued support throughout the season.

Labasa travels to play Nadi in the Extra Premier League competition this Sunday.

The match kicks off at 3pm at Nadi’s Prince Charles Park.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
